page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9a.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores, indicating a configuration of 1.0 thread per core. The processing power is delivered by an AMD EPYC 9T25 processor operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz.

This instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached options. The baseline network bandwidth for this instance is rated at 12 Gbps, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this server in the memory-optimized class, offering high memory density per compute resource. The lack of hyperthreading ensures predictable single-threaded execution per vCPU but limits concurrent thread density compared to hyperthreaded alternatives. The absence of local storage and GPUs makes it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that rely on network storage, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring consistent memory performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9T25 processor running at 2.7 GHz, with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration that provides dedicated physical execution units. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage solutions. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and data processing applications that require a high memory-to-CPU ratio without the need for local NVMe drives or hardware acceleration.
